topTargets.name: Makai Lemon
topTargets.reasoning: At 1.04, Lemon is the cleanest BPA if Love/Tate/Tyson are gone — Consensus #4 Tier 1 (PHI), ADP 11.18, RSP DoT 88.8. All three boards align, which is exactly what a low-RSP/high-ADP GM wants: no reach risk, consensus-anchored. WR depth (6) is fine but 7 contracts expiring means turnover is coming.

topTargets.name: Kenyon Sadiq
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #5 Tier 2 (NYJ TE1), ADP 13.11. RSP buries him at #30 but with only 10% RSP weight that barely registers — the 50/40 Consensus+ADP combo loves him. Roster has only 2 TEs, making this a clean need-meets-value pick at 1.04. NYJ landing spot (post-draft signal) is exactly the data a low-RSP GM trusts most.

topTargets.name: Jadarian Price
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #7 Tier 2 (SEA RB2), ADP 16.05, RSP #21. If Lemon and Sadiq are off the board at 1.04, Price becomes the consensus-safe RB swing. Only 4 RBs rostered and Price's SEA landing spot post-draft is the kind of confirmation low-RSP GMs anchor to.

topTargets.name: KC Concepcion
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #8 Tier 2 (CLE), ADP 13.57, RSP DoT 86.3 (#5). Strong triple-board overlap inside ADP top 20. Realistic 2.15 fallback if he slides — but more likely a stretch consideration at 1.04 only if Lemon/Sadiq/Price all gone.

topTargets.name: Caleb Douglas
topTargets.reasoning: Realistic 2.15 target — Consensus #27 Tier 4 (MIA WR15). Low-RSP GMs at 2.15 chase consensus names with NFL landing spots; Douglas fits with no RSP top-30 noise to distract. Late-round-2 dart at WR depth.

capPosture: Healthy at $36.2M used / $8.8M space — can absorb a 1.04 active-roster contract, no taxi-forcing required, but 7 expiring deals mean cap will get reshuffled regardless.

wildcard.name: Fernando Mendoza
wildcard.reasoning: Consensus #6 Tier 2 (LV QB1), ADP 11.39, RSP #7 — three boards all top-12. Already rostering 3 QBs makes it a stretch, but a low-RSP/ADP-driven GM staring at the consensus QB1 at 1.04 might bite on the name-brand value, especially if a starter contract is among the 7 expiring deals.

summary: Music City Mafia at 1.04 with low RSP affinity will anchor hard on Consensus and ADP overlap — meaning Makai Lemon is the heavy favorite if available, with Kenyon Sadiq (TE need + post-draft NYJ landing) the cleanest fallback. With 7 contracts expiring and only 18 roster spots filled, this is a refresh draft, not a rebuild — they want a Tier 1/2 contributor at 1.04 and a consensus-ADP name at 2.15, not a Waldman sleeper.
